## Overview

Amazon Web Services is the primary recommended hyperscaler for ArcKit as a Service Project 001 (managed SaaS). The eu-west-2 London region hosts the full Postgres / S3 / EKS / KMS / Bedrock stack the cell topology of ADR-001 / ADR-002 / ADR-006 is built on, and is the only UK region that natively co-locates Anthropic Claude 3.x and Amazon Nova for Category 2 AI provider needs.

## Products & Services

- **Compute**: EC2; ECS; EKS (managed Kubernetes); Lambda; Fargate.
- **Data**: RDS Postgres / Aurora Postgres (managed Postgres); S3 (object storage with Object Lock for immutable retention); DynamoDB (not in current shortlist).
- **AI**: Amazon Bedrock — multi-model inference platform hosting Anthropic Claude 3.5 Sonnet / Claude 3 Haiku / Amazon Nova / Llama-3 / Mistral in eu-west-2 London.
- **Identity / Security**: IAM; KMS (BYOK and external import); Secrets Manager; Certificate Manager; CloudTrail audit logging.
- **Observability**: CloudWatch Logs / Metrics; X-Ray traces.
- **Networking**: VPC; Direct Connect; PrivateLink.

## Pricing Model

Pay-as-you-go list rates with discounts:

- **1-year reserved / savings plan**: ~30% off list.
- **3-year reserved**: ~50% off list.
- **Egress** is metered; intra-region traffic free; cross-AZ charged.
- **Bedrock**: per-token, per-model. Claude 3.5 Sonnet ~£2.50/£12.00 per M (in/out); Claude 3 Haiku ~£0.20/£1.00; Amazon Nova Pro ~£0.65/£2.60; Llama-3.1-70B ~£0.55/£0.55 — UK eu-west-2 list rates as of 2026-05-03.

## UK Government Presence

- G-Cloud listed: yes (multi-lot).
- DOS listed: yes.
- UK data centres: yes — eu-west-2 London region with ≥3 AZs; second UK region availability via cross-region replication.

## Strengths

- UK eu-west-2 availability for the full ArcKit cell stack (Postgres, S3, EKS, KMS) and Bedrock with Anthropic Claude 3.x.
- UK GDPR Article 28 DPA; sub-processor inventory published; ISO 27001 / 27017 / 27018; SOC 1/2/3 Type II; NCSC Cloud Security Principles published assessment.
- Open primitives portable to Project 002 sovereign profile (S3 API → MinIO; Postgres wire → on-prem Postgres).
- KMS supports BYOK / external-key import for paid-tier customer-managed keys (NFR-SEC-004).
- RDS Postgres Multi-AZ has automatic failover and point-in-time recovery — RPO < 5 minutes.

## Weaknesses

- Hyperscaler concentration risk on operational dependency, even with open-standard primitives.
- CLOUD Act / Investigatory Powers Act exposure — must be assessed in DPIA; mitigated by CMK on paid tier and contractual notice obligations.
- Per-cell DB-instance floor cost (~£18k/year) sets the lower bound on cell economics.

## Projects Referenced In

- ArcKit as a Service Project 001 (managed SaaS) — primary recommendation in `ARC-001-RSCH-v1.0.md` Categories 1, 2, 4.
- ArcKit as a Service Project 002 (sovereign deployment) — referenced as the source pattern for primitives Project 002 self-hosts (S3 ↔ MinIO, RDS Postgres ↔ self-host Postgres).
